_README: Copy this file, rename it <your-book-id>.json, fill it in, then add a matching line to books.json (or run tools/build_manifest.py to regenerate the manifest automatically). Delete the _README and _notes keys when you're done — they're ignored by the app but keep the file tidy.
title: Book Title
author: Author Name
tagline: One punchy line shown on the shelf card.
journeyName: Name of the journey (eyebrow text on the map)
_notes_passThreshold: Fraction (0-1) of a level's questions needed to pass and unlock the next station. Default 0.6.

finalGrades.title: Lowest rank
finalGrades.blurb: Shown when the final-exam score is below the next tier.

_notes_finalGrades: Sorted by minPct ascending. The highest tier whose minPct <= the boss-exam score% is awarded.

levels.title: Level Title (shows on the board tile)

lessons.heading: Lesson card heading
lessons.body: Lesson text. Separate paragraphs with a blank line (\n\n).
lessons.quote: Optional pull-quote shown at the bottom of the card. Omit the key if not needed.

questions.q: Sort these into the right buckets.
questions.prompt: Drag each item into a bucket. On phone, tap an item then tap a bucket.

badge.name: Badge earned for passing this level

_notes_levels: The LAST level is treated as the boss/final exam — finishing it shows the final grade certificate instead of a normal reward screen. 'answer' is a 0-based index for mcq/scenario, and true/false for tf.
